phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 92.5 dBA, gradually settling to 65.5-73.5 dBA midway through session. Higher ambient noise detected; concentration requires active focus effort.
phase_1.vitals: Resting heart rate around 61 bpm observed, reflecting measured attention. Rate remained stable.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS positioning shows initial variance of 3.2m, refining to 2.9m accuracy over session duration. Movement sensors indicate rapid transition to stillness; no significant repositioning detected.

phase_2.correlations: Correlation analysis reveals moderate compatibility between positional, kinetic, and acoustic factors.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session demonstrates strong focus-supportive conditions with low ambient noise (73.5 dB), minimal body repositioning.

phase_3.recommendations: Modest noise control (e.g., ambient music, quiet background) could improve focus. Moderate session length adequate; potential to extend to 342s for skill building.

reason: Concentration score reflects good audio conditions (73.5dB), minimal motion, and brief session length.
